reviews.review_description: Truthfully, I feel like this game is criminally underrated. The story of the game is definitely one of the better ones I've played through. The music is one of the best parts of the game. And the designs, especially for the bosses are really unique. The way that the bosses looked alien yet still resembled a human was done very well. The difficulty I say was done farely well. It definitely gets harder but not unfairly so. The only negative thing I can say is that if you have buggy controller the game can be a bit hard to play but that's no fault of the game. If it got the right exposure I feel like it could become a pretty popular game. And I wish to see more stories unfold in this world

So I was looking for a game to help with my Shotwood obsession and this is it.
At the end of a day I dont always want to stand and dodge etc, its nice to sit and enjoy the immersion, and this game is great for that.
It's funny, visually slick, and real good fun to play.
I'm not entirely sure why other shooters get all the good press, I hadn't heard much about this game before but I'm really happy I bought it.
Honestly you won't regret it if you buy it too

I originally wasn’t very interested in this because it is on the rails. Nothing against on the rail games,it works great for cover shooters. I personally thought Blood & Truth on PSVR1 was incredible. This isn’t a cover shooter but the on the rails movement works well with the hectic gameplay. I love they gravity guns,they allow you to pick up random objects like rocks,doors and even mushrooms & pine cones that can then be launched at enemies. You can also grab enemies with the gravity gun,you can slam them into each other,slam them on the ground and even launch them at other enemies. What I find most satisfying is catching the projectiles enemies shoot at you and firing them back at an enemy. The gravity guns aren’t your only option though,you also have more traditional style guns too. I love the art style as well,it is very reminiscent of old school cartoons. The developers have created an interesting world as well as enemies to go with it. I definitely recommend this game,it is so much better than I originally imagined. I respect what the developers have created here. It is nothing like anything else I’ve played,it is both charming and original and it deserves more attention from the community
buy it

I got Harpagun after preparing fan art in the discord and got the game for free, and so here is my review of the game as of Version 1.0.0ea.
Harpagun, made by the Something Random studio, containing some of the great individuals who worked on SUPERHOT and SUPERHOT VR, once again delivered greatly in this version.
Harpagun retains and gives off the same, great unique charm as SUPERHOT while still being separate.
 The visual style of GORN, Wind Waker and Lethal company all feel like they were mashed into one in the game's VR, all deliver greatly. The visual style is amazing, and it all feels so good- It feels cartoony, but still grounded and doesn't come off as a generic sci-fi theme. The Visual artists and people behind the graphics and etc. have my applause.
The gameplay is very fun. Very fun. The loop and combat of killing enemies with powerful shotgun type weapons and machine guns and Half-Life 2 style gravity guns is all SUPER FUN.
The story, though very barebones as of this version, feels nice, simple and sweet. The bits of dialogue and interactions all feel so good.
As this is an early access, I would definitely invest money and time into it. With a discord available to input feedback in and with more content being planned on the horizon, I HIGHLY suggest to buy this game.

Loving the new release, looks amazing and plays great. My only issue is that haptics don't appear to be working at all? No controller rumble or any form of vibration when it comes to the game. Every other quest game has SOME kind of feedback from the controllers but the complete lack of that really strips some of the immersion. Not sure if it's just a bug but for now I'm giving it four stars instead of five due to that. Please fix when possible!

They had a sale going on for 9.99 so I decided to make the purchase. Actually was eyeing this game for awhile. This is a unique, action packed, chaotic time! The animation style is great and has a comic book vibe to it. The mechanics of your weapons work great. The ability to just about latch on to any object and use it as a weapon feels amazing! Good amount of Enemy variety. The movement system takes a bit to get use to since it's not your normal smooth locomotion system. Instead, it feels like a fast teleport type motion from spot to spot. It works well with the type of game this is. Also, this game can get very challenging. Many enemies will be coming at you from all sides so be prepared for battle! Along the way you also can get upgrades to your weapons which helps out a ton! The story is kinda "blah" but the game is still one hell of a ride. Happy I made the purchase and I'd strongly recommend this game to those still on the fence about it.

reviews.review_title: So fun it will ruin other shooters
reviews.review_description: This game is so friggin fun! You kinda dodge around to different spots, so it's kinda on rails, except it's not a straight line more like a network of different spots to jump between. You can shoot, charge a shot, and grab things in the environment and throw them and its feels great - like suck up a pile of stones and shoot them out like a machine gun - its wacky and funny and the gameplay is fantastic and the art is stylised and really good for a standalone quest game! The voice acting and music are also great! It's a rock solid title and its so satisfying to squish enemies and rip their heads off and figure out all the different ways to combat the monsters

reviews.review_title: The best arcade like game for quest
reviews.review_description: It's an arcade like game built for quest, plays like an on rails shooter, awesome boss battles, great story, an interesting art style, yes it's on early access, but the devs are active and Listening to critique and advice from the community, well worth it if they deliver on the promises on their roadmap, go buy it and enjoy

My first proper on rails shooter game in VR. I really enjoyed it! When I first booted up the game I was wondering why they chose the movement mechanic that they did but it makes sense when you get into it. It's a solid shooter game we'll worth checking out!

The last boss got me though I will admit. I couldn't figure it out but I did in the end. For those struggling, there are probably a million and own ways to do it but a little hint is that you can use the floating screen as a shield against the poison!

I really like the art style on this one and unlike a teleport only VR game, this one has kind of a smooth rail movement to it that isn’t too bad. The gravity gun mechanics are fun as you can grab some items to throw at the enemies, grab and throw the enemies themselves or rip parts off of them to expose weak points. The story is as linear as the rails you move around on and fairly short, about an hour and a half pending how good you are at it. You do get new enemies along the way and there are a couple boss fights. There are a couple different weapons that are introduced along the way, but you can only use them when they grant them to you and limited ammo use. You can also choose between the Arena and Arcade modes. Arena is just a small area that you ride the rails around on, in a circle, that continues to spawn enemies. Sometimes in there, you can literally sit in one spot and the enemies will just keep spawn and move in the exact same pattern. The arcade mode is basically the story mode path, with a score and timer. However, no leaderboards are shown to see how you compare. There were some minor kinks here and there, like I got stuck on a loading screen between levels, but a game restart fixed that. They also have a Discord server, so you can provide feedback in which they seem pretty receptive. It’s a fun short game with a bit of a rails only ‘Yupitergrad’ feel with the whole accent and art style.

Really fun experience. Reminds me of the old time crisis style of games where you move from point to point taking out the enemies along the way.  Different power ups make each encounter feel fresh and the multiple enemy types keep you on your toes.  Also allows for clever use of the environment to give you an advantage like holding a large log to block incoming attacks and moving behind a wall at the right time.  

If you're looking for a game with exploration, this isn't it however I definitely recommend it if you enjoy being taken on a fun ride.

I played Harpagun past the first boss fight. I recommend this game. The graphics, sound, humor, and shooting are fun. You get two gravity guns on each hand that can grab items from the environment or enemies and use them as shields, blunt weapons, or launch them as projectiles. The gravity guns are definitely the highlight of the game, and also have the ability to shoot energy projectiles. The movement system takes awhile to get used to. It is node based but you can move freely to available nodes which is very helpful in the group and boss fights. But don’t bounce between spots too much b/c you need to think about how to use the environment as a weapon and to block/dodge incoming attacks. Recommend setting your movement to left stick and rotation to the right stick with smooth turning set to the low speed.

A unique, fun, on rails movement system.

The grabbing controls are a little awkward to get used to, but they do work once you get the hang of it and particularly if you have better aim than I do! It has interesting mechanics where you need to grab some enemies to expose their weak spots, while dodging enemies and enemy shots using predetermined rail points that you move around to. 

The game is much harder than I was expecting it to be based on the art style (the first boss killed me a good dozen times as I learned its patterns), but it's a welcome, fair difficulty. Voice acting and music are great. 

I can't speak to the length as I haven't finished the game yet but I'm certainly enjoying my time in it.

I’ve only played about 45 mins so far… defeated the first boss. So this review is pretty early impressions.

Love the graphics style… simple yet detailed. 

The movement mechanics are dang near perfect. Real smooth and easy to move around. The difficulty feels just right too. 

My only complaint so far is I kinda figured there’d be an upgrade system… like upgrading the amount of ammo/charge in your arms? Maybe there is an upgrade system and I just haven’t reached that point yet?  If there isn’t one though, I really hope it’s added one day.  

Question for the dev: Are there any unlockables at all? 

I give this game a solid 8.5/10. If I come across an upgrade system of some kind though, it increases to a 9.5

reviews.review_title: I’ve played the first few levels
reviews.review_description:
As the title says, I’ve got through the first few levels. I set the settings to no blinders/blinkers (vignette) and the quality to the max. My QP and Q3 both play great with these settings. It’s a nice game to play standing up or sitting down. You quickly go from learning how to move around, to you’re killing things left, right and centre. The music, SE and GFX all bring you into the world and keep the pace going with you in the middle of all the action. One of the better games I’ve played on standalone VR, that forces you to keep your wits about you!

Highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: So much fun! New to VR games but love it
reviews.review_description:
Harpagun is a blast! It’s a fun, fast-paced shooter that keeps you engaged without the need to run around, making it perfect for sitting and playing comfortably. The on-rails movement works really well, letting me focus on the action without worrying about locomotion.

The graphics are surprisingly nice, adding to the immersive feel of the game. It starts off easy, giving you time to get used to the mechanics, but don’t be fooled—it ramps up quickly, and I love the challenge! If you’re into VR shooters and want something that keeps you on your toes without requiring a ton of movement, Harpagun is definitely worth checking out.

I would call the game a hybrid between a wave shooter and a traditional level based shooter.

It has this point by point teleporting system for movement that makes it unlike anything else I've played in VR.

The story campaign consists of levels that involve shooting, light puzzling and boss fights.
Whilst you have your guns to deal damage, you also have your magnetic grip to grab items in the scenes to use both offensively and defensively.

The Arena mode is essentially a wave shooter where you move around a central point.

The aim is just to survive 10 mins with your default guns and anything extra guns you may be rewarded with between rounds.
Thre are limited number of heart dotted around the arena to top up your health.

The Arcade mode seems to take the campaign level sections, removes the dialogue but adds in a scoring system to the mode (basically the arena mode in the main game settings)

Its a great game if your looking if you like your wave shooters but want something a little bit different (with movement, and the magnetic grip mechanic)
The dev seem to be highly focuses and have been open with the games development with a roadmap.

I can't wait to see how the game continues to grow and develop..as it is off to a very strong start already.

Gameplay is smooth and a lot of fun. You've got a decent variety of weapon power ups to pick up and the shooting feels smooth and responsive. Grabbing enemies or pieces of the environment to toss around is a blast that reminds me of the silly fun of grabbing the portal gun in Half-Life.

Music and voice acting are top notch. The dialogue had a great sense of humor. The graphics are cartoony but well done and immersive. Movement is node-based so you can play standing up or sitting down. Enjoyable game!

This game is a blast! It's a fast-paced shooter that tests your reflexes and lets you get creative in combat, all with a really cool art style. It's my go-to recommendation for friends trying VR for the first time, and they always love it!

reviews.review_title: Great game but currently over too soon
reviews.review_description:
I really like the presentation the audio is great  and the control system really works well. However for the asking price I feel it’s over too soon. I completed the campaign in just over 2hrs.  The campaign ends as if the story is going to continue so hopefully more content is being added.
During the training you are shown how to strafe but I couldn’t get this to actually work during game play (I fully accept I may have been doing it wrong). You can’t replay the training once completed so I couldn’t retry the strafing section. 
It’s a really great game and hopefully the developer keeps supporting it.
Game is very fun and reminds me of playing classic arcade games as a kid. Quest just doesn’t have a slot to put my quarters.

Fun addictive gameplay. Not breaking the mold on anything but the game is well put together. It's a good challenge but not punishing. Looking forward to seeing what the devs can cook up to really put this game on top
Good overall. Adjusting settings helps (vignettes off, anchoring off, quality to max). Pace and difficulty seems appropriate at default.
The game is great with cool mechanics and I'm enjoying the story! The controls can be a bit confusing, but I'm sure more gameplay will help with getting used to grabbing and shooting.
Art work was so good and the game was so fun! Definitely something to try.
